title: Silicularia polygaloides
taxonomy: Silicularia polygaloides is a species of lichen, belonging to the family Pilocarpaceae. It is a type of crustose lichen, which grows as a crust on rocks and other surfaces. The classification of Silicularia polygaloides includes its placement in the genus Silicularia, which is a group of small, usually crustose lichens. This genus is part of the family Pilocarpaceae, which includes several other genera of crustose lichens. The classification of Silicularia polygaloides is further detailed at the species level, with its binomial nomenclature being used to identify it specifically.
uses: Silicularia polygaloides is a type of plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections.
